content.answer:
Hi Ian and all,



Please find the attachment for Mar 20th KR/JP bi-weekly update vie email,
with agenda below for your reference. Feel free to let me know if you have
any questions, thank you.



   1.

   Overall Performance


   -

   P&L performance
   -

   KRJP LFF ADO and LFF% Monthly Actuals
   -

   Campaign participation
   -

   New seller acquisition progress



   2.

   For Discussion


   -

   KR RM KPI discussion



   3.

   For Updates


   -

   JP CC delay update
   -

   Counter Coupang update


Best,

Ray

content.question:
thanks

slide 11
- how is our ado in BR so far?
have we considered FBS for BR yet?

slide 12
- for VN, we are still unable to use local FBS?
still have to utilize LFF?

slide 16
- for #2 >> what is the issue around API integration right now?
- for #9 >> how are they doing right now?

slide 19
- how does the situation in JP look like?

- given that we will have alot of new hires, should we relook our
onboarding process as well?

slide 21 - 22
- what is the vendor working on from now till mid Apr to be able to improve
BWT?

Do we know what the key issue is right now, and what exactly is the
vendor trying to fix?

and do we know why it will take another month to fix
it?

- i remember we were looking at getting another CC vendor, where did we
land on that?

- did we proactively increase the EDT so that we do not disappoint on buyer
experience?

slide 31
- have we paid out the SLS + compensation yet?
- where did we land on getting compensation from vendor?

slide 36
- i thought we started to onboard F&B SKUs, how is the performance so far?
I remember we wanted to push F&B harder, but does not seem like much impact?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see our reponses below, feel free to let me know if you have any
questions.

 slide 11

- how is our ado in BR so far? have we considered FBS for BR yet?

   -

   BR Mar MTD ADO is 4,966(P6M CMGR 7%, 7% contri% to KR) and we explored
   BR 3PF option in ‘25 Jul but de-prioritized; 1) have less E2E cost
   competitiveness vs. SLS to sellers(5%p higher E2E cost) and 2) only white
   channel is available which also contributes to higher seller cost(e.g
   customs tax, license registration fee) from 1) not being able to
   under-value 2) license requirement

[image: image.png]


slide 12

- for VN, we are still unable to use local FBS? still have to utilize LFF?

-> yes, currently system dev KRSC <> VN local FBS is in progress(ETA ‘26
Q2-Q3), thus will push for FBS conversion right after. Conversion to FBS
expected to go smoothly as;

   -

   Better cost economics for seller: seller E2E cost lower, especially from
   16% lower WH cost vs. 3PF
   -

   Higher order conversion from more exposure and FBS tag


slide 16

- for #2 >> what is the issue around API integration right now?

   -

   For KR platform to onboard, API integration is essential for efficient
   OMS/WMS. Musinsa wants to check the complexity of API integration with SHP
   and currently waiting to receive their system requirements/specs to review
   with SHP BPM

- for #9 >> how are they doing right now?

   -

   Confirmed 2 brand (out of 100+) onboarding and in the process of
   onboarding more brands
   -

   Shop operation-wise, listing done but they have not started marketing
   yet as they plan to open offline store simultaneously. Will start
   promotions from Apirl with NSP participation


slide 19

- how does the situation in JP look like?

- given that we will have alot of new hires, should we relook our
onboarding process as well?

   -

   We prioritized KR test and will roll-out JP quiz after tailoring

Starting from Mar 15, shortening lead time from parcel scan in to export
   cc start to BAU level (2~3 days vs. currently 3~4 days) via new SOPs with
   3rd party vendors

Starting from Apr 1, negotiate with the export customs clearance agents
   for less frequent manifest inspection and more daily export clearance quota

We can only assess the impact of both (1) and (2) starting after Mar 24
   and mid-Apr if we consider that it is taking 12+ days from order placed to
   buyer delivery and because system tracking is limited from no line haul
   integration with Sagawa, we only know when parcel is scanned in the
   last-mile sorting center

- i remember we were looking at getting another CC vendor, where did we
land on that?

   -

   We invited 12 vendors  to submit their line haul/cc quotations on 3/20,
   aiming to decide on the final vendor by Apr 24, and go live with the new
   vendors Jul 1.
   -

   [RFQ & Go-live Timeline]

[image: image.png]

- did we proactively increase the EDT so that we do not disappoint on buyer
experience?

   -

   Yes, we are continuously increasing EDD/EDT by 10days(from Feb 11th for
   EDD) and will addt’l adjust EDD/EDT after monitoring the BWT this week as
   well

slide 31

- have we paid out the SLS + compensation yet?

   -

   The first round of the seller compensation is expected to be paid out to
   the sellers Apr W1(which is what we announced to sellers)
   -

   We expect that the compensation will be made on a rolling basis for
   orders placed late Jan~late Feb/early Mar, and subsequent compensations
   will be made weekly

- where did we land on getting compensation from vendor?

   -

   We are currently working with the legal team to define the scope of
   potential claims. The areas under review include:
   -

      Seller compensation payouts
      -

      SHP costs incurred, including operational expenses
      -

      Storage fees resulting from delays
      -

      Reputational damage (if applicable)
      -

   Additionally, we have aligned to submit the compensation claim later in
   Q2 to maintain an amicable relationship with Sagawa. This is particularly
   important as (1) we have not yet secured a new line haul vendor, and (2) we
   intend to continue us

[short term] Accelerating sku onboard of the top reseller varient.kr
   (capable of sourcing ~25% unmatched F&B SPUs) with ops support

[short term] Start manage CS F&B resellers (~400 GPs identified) with
   high potential
[mid term] Swarm project for mass SKU upload by end of May

[mid-long term] F&B brand/reseller acquisition, expecting 1 new F&B
   brand to onboard by H1


Appendix 1.

Metric

Jan

Mar

ADO

2.3

17.0

ADG

$93.8

$509.2

WL Shops

280

343

Managed shops

3

11

Live SKUs

3,540

3,771

ADO Efficiency

0.0006

0.0045

ADG Efficiency

$0.03

$0.14

content.question: Starting from Mar 15, shortening lead time from parcel scan in to
Starting from Apr 1, negotiate with the export customs clearance
We can only assess the impact of both (1) and (2) starting after Mar
[short term] Accelerating sku onboard of the top reseller varient.kr
[short term] Start manage CS F&B resellers (~400 GPs identified) with
[mid term] Swarm project for mass SKU upload by end of May
[mid-long term] F&B brand/reseller acquisition, expecting 1 new F&B

content.answer:
Starting from Mar 15, shortening lead time from parcel scan in to export
   cc start to BAU level (2~3 days vs. currently 3~4 days) via new SOPs with
   3rd party vendors

Starting from Apr 1, negotiate with the export customs clearance agents
   for less frequent manifest inspection and more daily export clearance quota

We can only assess the impact of both (1) and (2) starting after Mar 24
   and mid-Apr if we consider that it is taking 12+ days from order placed to
   buyer delivery and because system tracking is limited from no line haul
   integration with Sagawa, we only know when parcel is scanned in the
   last-mile sorting center


slide 21 - 22

There is a big gap between contract signing and go live?

Does this mean that we will drop Sagawa?

   -

   We plan to phase out Sagawa starting in July, while potentially
   retaining them for smaller markets (e.g., BR) to benefit from zero-rated
   tax treatment on transit warehouse services. From Apr 24 to July, we will
   use this period to test whether new vendors can meet our service
   requirements.


Previous Q&A

- i remember we were looking at getting another CC vendor, where did we
land on that?

   -

   We invited 12 vendors  to submit their line haul/cc quotations on 3/20,
   aiming to decide on the final vendor by Apr 24, and go live with the new
   vendors Jul 1.
   -

   [RFQ & Go-live Timeline]

[image: image.png]



slide 36

- Think we need faster acquisition of dedicated F&B seller/brands if we
want to do this properly.

1 by H1 feels very slow

   -

   Noted. In summary, a) 1 is secured but 5 additional brands in active
   discussion with high chance to convert; b) we will continue push hard for
   F&B seller acquisition but given structural difficulties, short-term
   progress will mainly come from existing reseller SKU sourcing; c) we will
   also source candidates with Coupang experience or F&B specialties to
   strengthen talent pipeline
   -

   Detailed next steps:

Continue push for brand acquisition:


   -

   Via SA:
   -

      Current list (86 brands, ~25% ttl CP brands): 1 confirmed, 5 in
      active talks, 41 pending, but initial 40 rejected given:
24 unreachable (60%)
14 diff biz strategy / no capacity (35%)

2 local distributor (5%)


   -

   Share brands’ CP performance to re-engage top-size rejected brands (ETA
   Apr W2), especially for reason 2)
   -

   Monthly refresh brand target list, working with TWBI to cover 100% F&B
   brands on CP by mid-year


   -

   Via SWARM (ERP): 1 confirmed, 2 in active talks, actively sourcing more
